goal: Integrate context graph into review workflow: auto-index on review, add blast-radius impact analysis to REVIEW.json, and expose via CLI graph-blast-radius command

tasks.name: Add review_impact() to ContextGraph
microSteps: Write tests for review_impact(): returns structured dict with blast radius for changed files, handles empty list, handles unknown files gracefully, aggregates impact across multiple files
Run failing tests to verify RED

Implement review_impact(changed_files) method: call self.index() for freshness, call self.impact() for each changed file, deduplicate affected nodes, return structured dict with affected_files, affected_symbols, max_depth, per_file breakdown
tasks.action: Add review_impact(self, changed_files: list[str]) -> dict method to ContextGraph. Steps: (1) call self.index() to ensure graph is fresh, (2) for each changed file, call self.impact(file_path) to get blast radius, (3) deduplicate affected nodes across files, (4) return dict with keys: graph_status ('indexed'), affected_files (sorted unique file paths from impact), affected_symbols (list of {name, label, file_path, depth}), per_file (dict mapping each changed file to its impact results), total_affected (count). Handle gracefully: empty changed_files returns empty structure, unknown files produce empty impact. Write 4 tests.

tasks.name: Integrate impact analysis into write_review() in workflow_checks_core.py
microSteps: Write tests for _graph_impact_section(): returns valid impactAnalysis dict when graph available, returns disabled dict when graph fails, handles no changed files
Run failing tests to verify RED
Implement _graph_impact_section(root, changed_relpaths) helper: import ContextGraph, try/except to handle missing graph gracefully, call graph.review_impact(), return structured dict for REVIEW.json
Wire _graph_impact_section into write_review() — call after invariants, add impactAnalysis key to contract dict
tasks.action: Add _graph_impact_section(root: Path, changed_relpaths: set[str]) -> dict function to workflow_checks_core.py. Implementation: (1) try to import ContextGraph and instantiate with root, (2) call graph.review_impact(sorted(changed_relpaths)), (3) wrap result in {enabled: true, ...impact_data}, (4) on any exception return {enabled: false, error: str(e)}. (5) Always call graph.close() in finally. Wire into write_review(): after tokens = summarize_token_telemetry(per_pkg), call _graph_impact_section(root, _changed_relpaths(root)) and add 'impactAnalysis' key to the contract dict before writing. Use _changed_relpaths(root) which already computes changed files. Write 3 tests using a temporary repo with Python files.

tasks.name: CLI graph-blast-radius subcommand
microSteps: Write tests for graph-blast-radius --help, empty repo, repo with dependencies showing impact, --json output, auto-detect changed files from git
Run failing tests to verify RED
Add graph-blast-radius argparse subparser with --repo, --files (optional comma-separated), --json flags
Implement cmd_graph_blast_radius handler: open graph, if --files provided split by comma, else detect changed files via git diff, call graph.review_impact(), print human or JSON output
Add graph-blast-radius to _graph_cmds set and dispatch dict
tasks.action: Add graph-blast-radius subcommand to workflow_memory.py. Argparse: --repo (path), --files (comma-separated file paths, optional), --json (flag). Handler cmd_graph_blast_radius: (1) open graph via _graph_open(repo), (2) if --files provided split by comma, else run git diff --name-only to detect changed files, (3) call graph.review_impact(files), (4) output results. Human format: header with file count, then per-file impact list with affected symbols and depth. JSON format: the review_impact dict directly. Add 'graph-blast-radius' to _graph_cmds set and dispatch dict. Write 5 tests.

commitMessage: feat(context-graph): integrate blast-radius impact analysis into review workflow and CLI
